Japanese: １０月１９日　午後３時２７分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: October 19, 3:27 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。今日は 危なかったですね、千尋さん‥‥。
English: That was a close one, huh, Mia?

Japanese: ふーん、そうなんだ。 ‥‥あたしも見たかったな。
English: Really? Too bad, I'm sorry I missed it.

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃん！
English: M-Maya!

Japanese: ね。ね。 ‥‥ケッキョク、どうなったの？
English: So, what happened?

Japanese: なんとなく、犯人の目星は ついてきたよ。
English: Well, I think I've got a pretty good idea who did it.

Japanese: あとは、証拠と‥‥動機、かな。
English: Now all I need is a motive... and proof.

Japanese: へえ！　すごいなあ！
English: Wow! Good going, Nick!

Japanese: “序審法廷”のシステムでは、 明日が最終日だからね。
English: Under the "initial trial" system, tomorrow is the last day we have.

Japanese: “序審法廷”？
English: "Initial trial"...? What's that?

Japanese: 聞いたことない？　２、３年前から 始まった法廷のシステム。
English: That's the new court system they introduced two or three years ago.

Japanese: 要するに、犯罪の件数が多いから、 サッサと片づけようっていう制度。
English: They had so many cases in the system, they decided to speed the whole process up.

Japanese: その制度だと、裁判って、３日で 終わっちゃうの？
English: So, under that system, trials have to end in three days?

Japanese: まあ、とりあえずはね。
English: Yeah, pretty much.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことだから、 さっそく、行こうか。
English: Well, we've no time to waste!

Japanese: 千尋さんのお気に入りだった 観葉植物だ。
English: Mia's favorite potted plant.

Japanese: 最近は、真宵ちゃんがさんざん 水をやるので、ふやけぎみだ。
English: Maya has been watering it so much lately it looks a little... swollen.

Japanese: かわいがっているつもりらしいが、 “水責め”に見えないこともない。
English: She's either trying to make it grow... or she's perfecting her water torture technique.

Japanese: 法律関係のムズカシイ本が イヤというほど並んでいる。
English: There's a horrendous amount of legal books here.

Japanese: これからは、ぼくが 読まなくちゃいけないのかな‥‥。
English: Scarier still is the thought that I have to read all of these now.

Japanese: 窓の外は、今日もいい天気だ。
English: It's a nice day outside.

Japanese: 最近、向かいの部屋は、 客の入らない夜はない。
English: The hotel across the way is always full these days.

Japanese: ボーイが楽しそうに 部屋をそうじしているのが見える。
English: There's the bellboy, happily cleaning the room.

Japanese: 千尋さんのデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: 今は、ぼくが使わせて もらっている。
English: Actually... it's my desk now.

Japanese: ここに座ると、どんなときでも 気持ちが引きしまる。
English: Sitting here always makes me feel like a professional.

Japanese: 千尋さんが好きだった、 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: A poster of an old movie that Mia liked.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみようかと 思ったのだが、
English: I was thinking I should go see it myself.

Japanese: このポスター、タイトルが 書かれてないぞ‥‥。
English: Then I realized that the poster doesn't actually say the title of the movie.

Japanese: なんでイブクロさん、
English: I don't get it, Maya.

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみなんて 盗んだのかなあ。
English: Why would Mr. Hammer steal the Steel Samurai costume?

Japanese: え！　あれって、 イブクロさんが入ってたの！
English: What!? You mean Mr. Hammer was wearing the costume!?

Japanese: そうなんだよ。
English: Yeah!

Japanese: で、でも、イブクロさんて、 被害者でしょ！
English: B-but Mr. Hammer was the victim, Nick!

Japanese: なんで、わざわざそんなことを？
English: Why would he go through the trouble of stealing it?

Japanese: その答えを、これから 見つけるんだよ。
English: That's what we have to find out.

Japanese: カントクたちの証言は どうなったの？
English: What happened with the director's testimony?

Japanese: カントクやプロデューサーたちが コテージにいたことは間違いない。
English: Well, it's pretty clear that the producer and the director were both in the trailer.

Japanese: そうなんだ‥‥。
English: Huh...

Japanese: でも、だからこそ、犯人は 彼らしかいないんだ。
English: Which means that the killer has to be one of them!

Japanese: えっ！　どうして！
English: Really!? Why?

Japanese: 殺人現場は、実はコテージのある、 第２スタジオだったんだよ。
English: Because the real scene of the crime was Studio Two... where that trailer is!

Japanese: うええええっ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: （ここまでおどろかれると、 なんかやりにくいなあ‥‥）
English: (Hmm... was Maya always this excitable?)

Japanese: なんか気づいたこと、ない？
English: Well, have you noticed anything that might help us?

Japanese: あたし、お姉ちゃんを呼び出して いるときは意識がないの。
English: I can't see what's going on when Mia is here...

Japanese: 気づきたくても、気づきようが ないんだ‥‥ごめんね。
English: So, no. I haven't noticed anything.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Right...

Japanese: ちょっと、ニボサブさんの話でも 聞きに行ってみようか。
English: Maybe we should go talk to WP?

Japanese: （ニボサブさんか‥‥）
English: (WP... Powers... right.)

Japanese: これ、どう？ ‥‥あの子の宝物だって。
English: What do you think about this? It's that fanboy's prized possession.

Japanese: うわ、すごい！
English: Wow! Impressive!

Japanese: でも、これ、いつもの トノサマンと、なんか違う。
English: It's a little different from the Steel Samurai on TV...

Japanese: まあ、デパートの屋上でやってる ショーの写真だからなあ。
English: These are mostly taken at those PR shows they do at stores and fairs...

Japanese: 着ぐるみの中に入っているのも、 荷星さんじゃないだろうし。
English: I'm pretty sure it's not even Will Powers inside that suit.

Japanese: ふっ。こんなニセトノサマンに マンゾクしているなんて。
English: No true fan would be satisfied with anything less than the real thing!

Japanese: まだまだ、彼もコドモだね。
English: He's still a child, I suppose...

Japanese: オトナは、ホンモノしかキョーミ ないもんね、なるほどくん！
English: I guess it takes an adult to appreciate the real deal. Right, Nick?

Japanese: そ、そんなもんかな。
English: Um, yeah, whatever you say.

Japanese: これ、九太くんの‥‥
English: This is Cody's--

Japanese: ああっ！　でじかめだ！
English: Hey! A digital camera!

Japanese: コ、コドモのクセに、 こんなモノを‥‥。
English: I'm impressed a kid like him would have something this expensive.

Japanese: さ、最近のコドモは、 ゼータクだよね！
English: Kids these days!

Japanese: （修行中の霊媒師は、どうやら 生活が苦しいようだ‥‥）
English: (I don't imagine spirit mediums-in-training get a lot of toys...)

Japanese: （まさか、これがイブクロさん だったとはね‥‥）
English: (Who would have thought that it was Jack Hammer inside!)

Japanese: （問題は、イブクロさんがなぜ、 こんなことをしたか、だ‥‥）
English: (But why did he steal it? That's what I have to figure out.)

Japanese: あたしもほしいな。 トノサマン・スピアー。
English: I wish I had my own Samurai Spear!

Japanese: なるほどくんを 思うさまつついてみたい。
English: Just think, I could poke you as much as I wanted!

Japanese: な、なんでぼくなんだよ‥‥。
English: Me!? Why me!?

Japanese: ‥‥それがどうかした？ 何も思いつかないけど。
English: Sorry, I don't know how that will help us figure this out.

Japanese: １０月１９日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: October 19 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。 今日もイカしてましたね。
English: Mr. Wright! You did very well again today... thank you!

Japanese: あ、ありがとうございます。
English: Oh... thanks.

Japanese: ‥‥その、うるんだヒトミは やめてください。
English: Um, could you not look like you're about to burst into tears...?

Japanese: へえ。なるほどくん、 イカしてたんだぁ。
English: Wow, Nick! You must have really wowed 'em in there.

Japanese: ‥‥その、失礼なニヤケヅラも やめてほしいな。
English: You could stop looking so... vulnerable, too.

Japanese: ニボサブさん！　とにかく、 残すは明日、１日だけです。
English: Hey, WP! Only one day left!

Japanese: がんばりましょうね！
English: Let's make it count!

Japanese: はあ。
English: Yes...

Japanese: ‥‥といって、ボクに何ができる というワケでもないんですけど。
English: Though there's not really anything I can do to help.

Japanese: あはは。ま、あたしも そうなんですけどねー。
English: Hah hah hah! I'm not really sure what I can do, either.

Japanese: “あはは”じゃないだろ。
English: Please... this is no laughing matter.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 荷星さんの迫力にビビって 動けないみたいだ。
English: He seems frozen in awe of Will Powers.

Japanese: 姫神さんのこと、 何か話してもらえますか。
English: Could you tell me more about Ms. Vasquez?

Japanese: ああ‥‥、 あのプロデューサーの方ですか。
English: Oh... the producer?

Japanese: あの人は、業界で“天才”と 言われている人です。
English: She's well respected in the industry. They say she's a genius.

Japanese: “テンサイ”‥‥。
English: A genius...?

Japanese: 英都に来てから、もう ５年ぐらいたちますけど‥‥。
English: She's been at Global Studios for five years now.

Japanese: 今じゃあ、あの人には 誰もアタマが上がりません。
English: Right or wrong, nobody dares tell her different these days.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: Why is that?

Japanese: あの人は、ここへ来てから たてつづけにヒット作を作って、
English: Ever since she came on board, we've had nothing but hits.

Japanese: 当時つぶれかけていた英都を、 ミゴトよみがえらせたんです。
English: Global Studios was on its knees, but she picked it up and made it shine.

Japanese: それに‥‥。
English: Oh, and...

Japanese: それに‥‥なんですか？
English: And... what?

Japanese: いいえ。なんでもないです。
English: N-nothing. Forget it.

Japanese: ‥‥ボクも、ウワサしか 知らないんで‥‥。
English: I... I've only heard rumors, anyhow.

Japanese: 宇在カントクって、 どんな人ですか？
English: Can you tell me anything about Mr. Manella?

Japanese: あの人は、これまでマイナーな ビデオ作品ばかり作ってたんです。
English: Oh, he used to be a minor straight-to-video director.

Japanese: それを、あの姫神プロデューサーが 目にとめて、
English: But something in his work caught Vasquez's eye.

Japanese: トノサマンの話を 持っていったんです。
English: She brought the Steel Samurai idea to him.

Japanese: おかげで、宇在カントクは 今じゃ有名人です。
English: Now everyone knows the name Sal Manella.

Japanese: カントク、プロデューサーには アタマが上がらないみたいですよ。
English: I hear he's pretty much at her beck and call, though.

Japanese: 彼女の言うことなら、なんでも ハイハイ聞いてます。
English: Whenever she says "jump!" he asks "how high?" and all that.

Japanese: （うーん、目に浮かぶなあ‥‥）
English: (I can imagine him being at her beck and call... though I can't imagine him jumping.)

Japanese: あの、イブクロさんのこと なんですけど。
English: I was wondering about Mr. Hammer...

Japanese: ムカシはあの人、大スターだった じゃないですか。
English: He was a big star back in the day, right?

Japanese: 急に名前を聞かなくなっちゃった んですけど‥‥。
English: But then he just dropped off the face of the Earth.

Japanese: ああ‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ボクも、あの人にアコガレて この世界に。
English: Yes, you're right. In fact, he was my role model when I started in this business.

Japanese: あの人、急に大きなシゴトを しなくなったんですよ。
English: But he just stopped taking on big roles.

Japanese: そして、この英都で 小さな作品に出るように‥‥。
English: That's when he began appearing in little productions at Global...

Japanese: ええ！　どうしてなんですか？
English: How could he do that!?

Japanese: あたし、“武者ぶるいの夏”の 続編、たのしみにしてたんですよ！
English: I was really looking forward to a sequel to "Samurai Summer."

Japanese: イブクロさん、スターの座を下りた のが、５年ぐらい前なんですよ。
English: He just gave up being a star five years ago.

Japanese: ‥‥考えてみると。
English: Actually, now that I think about it...

Japanese: ちょうどそのころなんですよね。 ‥‥姫神さんが英都に来たの‥‥。
English: That was right when Ms. Vasquez came to Global.
(Five years ago...?)

Japanese: 荷星さん。これ‥‥。
English: Have you seen this, Mr. Powers?

Japanese: な、なんですかこれ。
English: W-what's that!?

Japanese: ‥‥身におぼえのないトノサマンが たくさんならんでいますケド‥‥。
English: I've never seen these Steel Samurais in my life...

Japanese: 遊園地とかのショーに出ている トノサマンです。
English: These are the Steel Samurais that appear at live shows.

Japanese: ファンの男の子が、たった１人で 集めたんですよ。
English: A fanboy collected all these pictures himself.

Japanese: こういうものを見ると、
English: Seeing things like this really makes me think...

Japanese: “ガンバラなきゃ！” って、思います‥‥。
English: I mean so much to these kids, you know? *sniff*

Japanese: 凶器のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: Regarding the murder weapon...

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: 重いんですか？　あれ。
English: Is it heavy, that spear?

Japanese: けっこう重いですよ。
English: It's pretty hefty, yeah.

Japanese: ふつうの人は、自由にふり回すのは ムリだと思いますよ‥‥。
English: I don't think an average person would be able to swing it around effectively.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: なんか、なんか、午前中の 打ち合わせで壊れた、とか‥‥？
English: And... that's the spear that broke or something during the morning run-through?

Japanese: ええ。ボク、回しゲリを シッパイしちゃって‥‥。
English: Oh yes, I goofed on a round kick, you see...

Japanese: スピアーを、 折っちゃったんですよねぇ。
English: I broke the spear.

Japanese: とりあえず、オバチャンが なおしてくれましたけど。
English: The security lady fixed it for me right away though.

Japanese: ガムテープで、ね。
English: With duct tape. We heard.

Japanese: この写真ですけど‥‥。
English: Here's that picture again.

Japanese: ホ、ホントに、着ぐるみの中には イブクロさんが‥‥？
English: I-is that really Jack Hammer inside that suit?

Japanese: そうとしか考えられないんです。
English: He's the only one who it could have been.

Japanese: でも、どうしてそんなことを？
English: But why would he...?

Japanese: そうだよ！　どうして？
English: Yeah! Why?

Japanese: 霊媒師だろ。 ‥‥呼び出してよ、イブクロさん。
English: You're the medium! Why don't you summon up Mr. Hammer's spirit and ask him yourself?

Japanese: うう‥‥。 あと３年、待って。
English: *sigh* Gimme about three more years.

Japanese: （やれやれ。おたがい、 半人前、か‥‥）
English: (Look at us, the two novices.)

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: E-excuse me.

Japanese: せっかく、そのようなものを つきつけていただいても、
English: Thank you for showing me that, whatever it is.

Japanese: モウシワケありませんが、 心あたりがございません。
English: I wish I could think of some way for me to help you...

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: S-sorry. *sniff*

Japanese: １０月１９日　某時刻 英都撮影所・正門前
English: October 19 Global Studios Main Gate

Japanese: オバチャン、今日は しずかだね。
English: The old windbag sure is quiet today.

Japanese: いつもこうだと、 この撮影所も平和なのに。
English: You know, this place is really... peaceful, like this.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick?

Japanese: ちょっと、ハリアイがないね。
English: I think her guard's down...

Japanese: ま、まあね。
English: Y-yeah?

Japanese: 今なら、詰所のようかん、 食べてもだいじょうぶかも。
English: I bet we could even eat those donuts in the guard station... if we wanted.

Japanese: ‥‥食べたら、ハッたおす。
English: You eat, you die.

Japanese: あ。よかった、生きてたよ。
English: Whew! She's alive!

Japanese: この撮影所の正面入口だ。
English: The main entrance to the studios.

Japanese: 今日も、見張りの警官が 立っているはずだ。
English: They probably have a police officer on patrol out there.

Japanese: この撮影所のライトバンだ。
English: Looks like a studio van.

Japanese: 撮影用の資材などを 運んでいるのだろう。
English: They probably use it to carry around film equipment.

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: オバチャンがむさぼるのだろう。 テーブルの上は、おかしの山だ。
English: Ms. Oldbag must like junk food. There's a mountain of it on the desk in there.

Japanese: セキュリティを管理する コンピュータが見える。
English: I can see the computer that runs the security cameras.

Japanese: この撮影所の見取り図が 描かれている。
English: An overhead map of the studios.

Japanese: ここから右へ行くと、 《スタッフ作業場》
English: Right from here is the Employee Area.

Japanese: 左へ行くと、《スタジオ方面》に 通じているらしい。
English: To the left are the actual film studios.

Japanese: 撮影所内のセキュリティカメラの 映像を管理するコンピュータだ。
English: The computer that runs the studio security cameras.

Japanese: ‥‥おや、今日は スイッチが入っていないみたいだ。
English: Huh? It looks like it's been turned off for the day.

Japanese: あの‥‥姫神プロデューサーの ことですけど‥‥。
English: Um, I was wondering about Dee Vasquez, the producer.

Japanese: 英都のおエラ方は、あの女を 気に入ってるからね。
English: Oh, the studio bigwigs LOVE her.

Japanese: あの女の思いどおりにならない ことは、ないョ。
English: So she always gets her way.

Japanese: （ニガニガしい口調だ‥‥）
English: (She seems bitter...)

Japanese: キライなんですか？ プロデューサーのこと？
English: Do you... not like the producer?

Japanese: あの女のことは話せないョ。 ‥‥撮影所の人から言われたから。
English: Look, the studio people don't want me talking about her, so, sorry.

Japanese: 宇在カントクですけど‥‥。
English: Can I ask you about Sal Manella, the director?

Japanese: アイツは、姫神の言いなりに動く、 フヌケた男だよ。
English: Sal? He's soft--a pushover. Does whatever Vasquez tells him to.

Japanese: どうも、姫神にイジメられるのが、 ウレシイみたいだネ。
English: She treats him like dirt, but I think he likes it.

Japanese: えー？　なんでイジメられるのが ウレシイんですか？
English: Huh? Why would anyone like being treated like dirt?

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。なんで？
English: That doesn't make any sense, does it, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥さ、さあねえ。
English: Er... No, no it doesn't, Maya.

Japanese: イブクロさんのことですけど‥‥。
English: About Mr. Hammer...

Japanese: アンタ！
English: Arrgh!

Japanese: 今日はよくも、イイカゲンなことを 言ってくれたネ！
English: I heard about what you said today! Cheeky whippersnapper!

Japanese: あの、あの大スターのイブクロ ちゃんを、こともあろうに‥‥
English: Poor old Hammer! You'd drag his star down from the sky and stomp on it!

Japanese: ハンザイシャ呼ばわり するなんて‥‥
English: Calling him a criminal! A thief!

Japanese: オ、オバ、オバチャン、 許さないよぉ！
English: I-I-I-I... I won't forget this!!!

Japanese: （ホ、ホンキで怒ってるぞ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh, she's really pissed this time!)

Japanese: いいかい！ イブクロちゃんはねェ！
English: You listen to me, whipper- snapper! My poor old Hammer would never do such a thing!

Japanese: オバチャンの目をごまかすために、 荷星の着ぐるみを盗むような、
English: Why would he steal Powers's costume so he could sneak by me?

Japanese: そんなヒキョウなことは ゼッタイ、しないんだ！
English: He would never stoop so low! It's impossible!

Japanese: あの、オバチャン、これ‥‥。
English: Um, Ms. Oldbag?

Japanese: そんなモノ、見せるんじゃないョ！
English: What!? Get that filth out of my sight!

Japanese: それがイブクロちゃんだって？
English: And you're claiming that's poor old Hammer in there!?

Japanese: デタラメもいいとこだよ！
English: And I thought you were such an innocent youth... Bah!

Japanese: あの、オバチャン、これ‥‥。
English: Um, Ms. Oldbag? What do you think of this?

Japanese: ‥‥今は、何も 考える気にならないョ‥‥。
English: Think? I'm not in the mood for thinking, thank you very much.

Japanese: なんだいアンタたち。 まだいたのかい。
English: What, you're still slinking around?

Japanese: そんなにここが好きなら、 オバチャンのかわりに働くかい？
English: If you like the place so much, why don't you take over for me?

Japanese: オバチャン‥‥ 元気ないですね。
English: The old windbag doesn't look so good...

Japanese: みんな、イブクロちゃんのことを わすれようとして、必死だョ。
English: E-everybody's doing their darnedest to forget Hammer.

Japanese: 英都撮影所がここまで大きく なったのは、誰のオカゲだい？
English: Who do they think made Global Studios what it is today?

Japanese: みーんな、イブクロちゃんの オカゲじゃないか‥‥。
English: Hammer! It's all due to Hammer!

Japanese: オ、オバチャンが グチをこぼしてる‥‥。
English: She's starting to froth at the mouth, Nick...

Japanese: キケンだよ、なるほどくん。
English: Maybe we should keep a safe distance?

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ５年前のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: I wanted to ask you about five years ago...

Japanese: アンタ！ ‥‥ダレに、それを！
English: Who told!? Who did you hear that from!?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥。
English: I, uh... huh?

Japanese: 間宮だね！
English: Nichols!

Japanese: ま、まみや‥‥？
English: N-nickels?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 スタッフの子の名前だよ。
English: Nichols, Nick. Penny Nichols. That assistant we talked to.

Japanese: と、とにかく。
English: O-oh, right.

Japanese: 聞いたんですよ。
English: Anyway, we heard about the accident.

Japanese: イブクロさん、撮影中の事故で、 人を‥‥
English: It was during filming with Jack Hammer...

Japanese: その。 ‥‥死なせちゃった、って。
English: He killed a man. Didn't he?

Japanese: アンタたち！
English: Wh-whippersnappers!

Japanese: そうやって、人のムカシを ほじくりかえして、
English: Dredging up dirt on someone's past like that... and the recently departed, no less!

Japanese: たのしいかい！
English: I suppose you think this is fun!?

Japanese: い、いや。これは、荷星さんを 助けるためですから‥‥。
English: N-no, I'm just doing my job to protect Mr. Powers...

Japanese: イブクロちゃんが、荷星の 着ぐるみを盗んだって！
English: And you claim that Hammer stole Powers's costume!

Japanese: そんな言いがかり、 オバチャンは信じないよ！
English: You expect me to believe that rubbish!?

Japanese: それとも、何か証拠でも あるのかい！
English: Or do you have some kind of proof?

Japanese: （イブクロさんが着ぐるみを盗もう とした証拠か‥‥どうだろう？）
English: (Proof that Hammer stole Powers's costume? Hmm...)

Japanese: 証拠は‥‥ありませんけど。
English: Proof? Well... no.

Japanese: ほら、ごらんョ！
English: See! See!?

Japanese: ツゴウの悪いことを、ゼンブ 死んだ人に押しつけようなんて。
English: I see how you work! Anything that doesn't fit, you just blame on the dead!

Japanese: そ、そんなこと‥‥ やめておくれ！
English: Well... well... Just butt out, lawyer-boy!

Japanese: （‥‥このオバチャンは、 やはり何かを知っている！）
English: (She knows something!)

Japanese: （なんとしてでも 聞き出さなきゃな‥‥）
English: (But how am I going to get her to talk...?)

Japanese: オバチャンには悪いけど‥‥ あるんですよ、証拠。
English: I'm sorry Ms. Oldbag, but I do have proof.

Japanese: イブクロさんが、着ぐるみを 盗もうとしたという証拠は‥‥、
English: I can prove that Mr. Hammer stole Mr. Powers's costume.

Japanese: フン！ なんだい、そんなもの！
English: Hmph! What's that supposed to prove!?

Japanese: オバチャンは認めないよ。
English: That means nothing to me!

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥あたしにも 意味がわからないよ。
English: Nick... I'm kinda having trouble figuring that one out too.

Japanese: （‥‥これはどうも、 違ったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... wrong evidence?)

Japanese: （‥‥でも、このオバチャンは 何かを知っている！）
English: (But I know Oldbag knows something!)

Japanese: （なんとしてでも 聞き出さなきゃな‥‥）
English: (I have to get her to talk!)

Japanese: これが証拠です。
English: Here's my proof.

Japanese: なんだい、その セコいビンは‥‥？
English: What? What's a little empty bottle supposed to prove?

Japanese: 睡眠薬です。
English: It's a bottle of sleeping pills.

Japanese: ‥‥イブクロさんの 指紋がついています。
English: With Mr. Hammer's fingerprints on it...

Japanese: そ、それがどうしたんだい！
English: Wh-what does that prove?

Japanese: イブクロちゃんだって、 眠れないコトはあるサ！
English: I... I'm sure old Hammer had some sleepless nights!

Japanese: そのクスリを、荷星に使った っていう証拠はあるのかい！
English: Where's your proof that he used those pills on Powers?

Japanese: ‥‥あの日、おヒルに ホネつきステーキを食べましたね？
English: Mr. Powers ate a t-bone steak for lunch, correct?

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥食べたケド。
English: W-well... yes. So?

Japanese: これは、そのときの 荷星さんの皿です。
English: This is the plate that he used to eat that steak.

Japanese: 睡眠薬のコンセキが 見つかっています。
English: There are traces of sleeping pill powder on the plate!

Japanese: ‥‥あの日、おヒルに ホネつきステーキを食べましたね？
English: Mr. Powers ate a t-bone steak for lunch, correct?

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥食べたケド。
English: W-well... yes. So?

Japanese: これは、そのときの 荷星さんの皿です。
English: This is the plate that he used to eat that steak.

Japanese: 睡眠薬のコンセキが 見つかっています。
English: There are traces of sleeping pill powder on the plate!

Japanese: は‥‥はん！
English: Mmmph? Hah!

Japanese: 荷星が自分で 入れたんじゃないのかい！
English: I-I bet Powers put it in there himself!

Japanese: あんな男でも、眠れないこと ぐらい、あるだろうからね。
English: Even a lightweight like him must have trouble sleeping sometimes.

Japanese: この睡眠薬、イブクロさんが 入れたという証拠があるんですよ。
English: Except, I have proof that Mr. Hammer put the pills in.

Japanese: 睡眠薬が入っていたビンです。
English: This bottle contained sleeping pills.

Japanese: ‥‥イブクロさんの指紋が 残っていました。
English: Mr. Hammer's fingerprints were found on the bottle.

Japanese: そうかい‥‥。
English: I see.

Japanese: かわいそうにねえ‥‥ イブクロちゃん。
English: Poor, poor Hammer.

Japanese: やっちゃいけないコト、 ‥‥やっちゃったんだネェ。
English: You did wrong, Hammer, rest your soul.

Japanese: オバチャン‥‥。
English: Ms. Oldbag...?

Japanese: アンタにゃ負けたよ。 ‥‥話すとしようか。
English: Okay. You win. I'll talk.

Japanese: オバチャンも、ちょっと つかれちゃったからね‥‥。
English: I'm tired, see... Tired of holding it all in.

Japanese: オバチャン‥‥。
English: Ms. Oldbag...

Japanese: たしかに、今から５年前。 ‥‥人が死ぬ事故があったよ。
English: You're right. Five years ago there was an accident... a fatal accident.

Japanese: まずいことに、芸能記者が 写真を撮っちまってね。
English: What's worse, a paparazzi took a photo of it.

Japanese: その写真をめぐって、大さわぎさ。
English: That photo, well, it caused quite a stir.

Japanese: そのさわぎをおさめたのが あの女、姫神だよ。
English: And guess who made it all better? Vasquez!

Japanese: あいつ、暴力団とつながりが あってさ。
English: She has ties to the mafia...

Japanese: 芸能記者たちを、だまらせたんだ。
English: She silenced that paparazzi.

Japanese: ‥‥それからだョ。
English: That was the beginning.

Japanese: あの女が、英都でデカいカオを するようになったのは‥‥。
English: After that, she became a force to be reckoned with here at the studio.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったんですか。
English: I see...!

Japanese: でも。わかってやってョ。
English: But, you have to understand...

Japanese: イブクロちゃんに、 悪気はなかったんだ！
English: Poor old Hammer never meant any harm to anyone!

Japanese: オバチャン‥‥。
English: Ms. Oldbag...

Japanese: ちょっと待ってな。
English: Hold on a minute.

Japanese: なんか、オバチャン かわいそう‥‥。
English: Poor Ms. Oldbag...

Japanese: これ、持っていきな。
English: Here, take this.

Japanese: ？　‥‥写真、ですか。
English: A photograph?

Japanese: こ‥‥‥これは！
English: W-wait a second!

Japanese: そのときの写真だよ。
English: This is the picture.

Japanese: ここは‥‥第２スタジオの コテージ、ですか‥‥？
English: Is... this the trailer in Studio Two?

Japanese: イブクロちゃん、階段の上で ワルモノと戦うシーンでね。
English: Hammer was supposed to fight with a bad guy on the top of those stairs, there.

Japanese: 相手役を‥‥花壇の鉄柵の 上に‥‥。
English: He pushed the other actor, and the man fell onto the flowerbox fence.

Japanese: ‥‥なんでオバチャンが、 こんなものを‥‥！
English: B-but how did you get this?

Japanese: ムカシのことだよ。 ‥‥話したくもないネ。
English: It... was a long time ago. I don't feel much like talking about it.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: I understand...

Japanese: 《５年前の事故の写真》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Five-Year-Old Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: たしかに、今から５年前。 ‥‥人が死ぬ事故があったよ。
English: You're right. Five years ago there was an accident... a fatal accident.

Japanese: まずいことに、芸能記者が 写真を撮っちまってね。
English: What's worse, a paparazzi took a photo of it.

Japanese: その写真をめぐって、大さわぎさ。
English: That photo, well, it caused quite a stir.

Japanese: そのさわぎをおさめたのが あの女、姫神だよ。
English: And guess who made it all better? Vasquez!

Japanese: あいつ、暴力団とつながりが あってさ。
English: She has ties to the mafia...

Japanese: 芸能記者たちを、だまらせたんだ。
English: She silenced that paparazzi.

Japanese: それからだョ。
English: That was the beginning.

Japanese: あの女が、英都でデカいカオを するようになったのは‥‥。
English: After that, she became a force to be reckoned with here at the studio.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったんですか。
English: I see...!

Japanese: でも。わかってやってョ。
English: But, you have to understand...

Japanese: イブクロちゃんに、 悪気はなかったんだ！
English: Poor old Hammer never meant any harm to anyone!

Japanese: オバチャン‥‥。
English: Ms. Oldbag...

Japanese: ５年前の、その事故‥‥。
English: Poor old Hammer. He was a big star. The biggest.

Japanese: 当時、大スターだった イブクロちゃんは、
English: That accident five years ago is what did him in.

Japanese: なんとしても、 かくしておきたかったんだよ。
English: He wanted to hide it. He didn't want anyone to know.

Japanese: バカな子だよ。 スナオに公表しておけば‥‥。
English: Poor, silly old Hammer. If he had just come out and confessed...

Japanese: 姫神なんかに利用されずに すんだのにネェ‥‥。
English: Then Vasquez wouldn't have gotten her claws into him!

Japanese: ‥‥ごめんョ。
English: Sorry...

Japanese: もう、この事件のことは 考えたくもないんだョ。
English: I don't want to think about it anymore.

Japanese: １０月１９日　某時刻 撮影所・スタッフエリア
English: October 19 Global Studios Employee Area

Japanese: 撮影は今日も中止、か。
English: I guess they're not filming today, either.

Japanese: １回ぐらい、見てみたかったな。
English: I did want to see them filming... just one time.

Japanese: 明日の法廷で決着をつけるから、 それから見に来ればいいよ。
English: This trial will be over and done by tomorrow. You can come see them film later.

Japanese: そうだね‥‥。
English: I guess...

Japanese: スイミンヤクのビンって、 ここで見つけたんだよね？
English: This is where you found that bottle of sleeping pills, right?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Uh huh.

Japanese: 他にも、何かないのかな‥‥。
English: I wonder if there're any other clues lying around.

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおっ！
English: Yaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaarh!

Japanese: ダメッス！ さわっちゃダメッス！
English: No no no! No touchin'!

Japanese: きゃああああああっ！
English: Eeek!

Japanese: いや、スマンッス。
English: Oh, sorry, pal.

Japanese: もしかしたら、おどろかせて しまったかもしれないッスな。
English: Didn't mean to frighten you.

Japanese: も、もしかしなくても おどろきましたッ！
English: D-didn't mean...? I almost had a heart attack!

Japanese: いやいや。
English: Heh heh. I guess I can be a little dramatique at times...

Japanese: たまには自分も、ドラマティックに キメてみたいッス。
English: I suppose it's the actor in me...

Japanese: （“ティック”ときたか‥‥）
English: ("Dramatique" indeed...)

Japanese: コホン。
English: Ahem.

Japanese: とにかく、ちょっとそのテーブルの 皿を調べるッス。
English: Anyway, I'm here to examine the plate on that table.

Japanese: もしかして、睡眠薬の？
English: Wait, are you looking for sleeping pills?

Japanese: そッス。 コンセキを調べるッス。
English: Yeah, pal. Traces thereof.

Japanese: “お茶”“みそ汁”“おしるこ” “くず湯”“おでん”‥‥
English: Let's see... cola, candy, chips, gum...

Japanese: ラインナップが 和風に統一されている。
English: Huh. It's so... normal.

Japanese: 楽屋の入口に、 張り紙がしてある。
English: There's a sign on the dressing room door.

Japanese: “荷星　三郎様”か。 ‥‥さすが、主役だな。
English: "Mr. Will Powers"... it must be neat to be the star and have your own room like this.

Japanese: 楽屋に入るドアだ。 カギはかかっていない。
English: The door to the dressing room. It's not locked.

Japanese: 楽屋とそとをむすぶ窓だ。
English: A window into the dressing room.

Japanese: くもりガラスなので、 部屋の中を見ることはできない。
English: The glass is frosted, so I can't see inside.

Japanese: 巨大な倉庫だ。
English: That must be the storage room.

Japanese: 撮影用の大道具が しまってあるようだ。
English: That's where they keep all the film equipment.

Japanese: モーターを乗せた、 電動自転車だ。
English: Looks like one of those electric-powered bicycles.

Japanese: なんともいえない中途ハンパな 感じが、ぼくはキライだ。
English: I dunno about these. I mean, what's the point?

Japanese: えー。でも、坂をのぼるの ラクだよ。
English: It makes it a lot easier to go up hills!

Japanese: 若いのに、あんなもの使うな！
English: Don't people cycle to get exercise!?

Japanese: ‥‥ほったらかしたままだな。
English: That grate's still lying here.

Japanese: まあ、たとえ修理してあっても、
English: Ah, they've seen wisdom at last!

Japanese: また真宵ちゃんが ぶっコワすけどね。
English: They know if they fixed it I'd just kick it off again.

Japanese: スタッフの子が泣くぞ‥‥。
English: That poor assistant...

Japanese: 排水口にハマっていた 金網だ。
English: The grate that used to cover the drain.

Japanese: ちょっと曲がっている。 ハメなおすのはムリだな。
English: It's a little bent. I don't think it would fit if I tried to put it back on.

Japanese: スタッフたちの、 昼食のあとだ。
English: The remains of the employees' lunch are scattered around.

Japanese: 事件があったため、 誰も片づけていないのだろう。
English: Everyone was probably too shocked to clean up.

Japanese: 皿の上には、ホネつきステーキの ホネだけが乗っている。
English: There's a t-bone on one of the plates, minus the steak.

Japanese: 描きかけの書き割りが 立てかけてある。
English: There's a half-finished backdrop for a stage here.

Japanese: ネオ・エドシティの城 ということは、
English: Looks like a castle.

Japanese: おそらく“ネオ・エドジョー” とでもいうのだろう。
English: Probably "Neo Olde Tokyo Castle."

Japanese: 睡眠薬のコンセキを調べる‥‥ って、どうするんですか？
English: So you're looking for traces of the sleeping pills... how?

Japanese: くわしいことは 持ち帰って調べるッスけど、
English: For a thorough examination I gotta take the plate back to the precinct.

Japanese: 今ここで、カルく調べることが できるようにと、
English: But I have the capabilities to make a cursory examination right here!

Japanese: 鑑識のセンセイが、 試薬をくれたッス。
English: Our science guy gave me some testing solution.

Japanese: 反応があれば、クスリの色が 変わるッス。
English: This reacts to sleeping pills, see? If there're traces it'll change color.

Japanese: わぁ、オモシロそう！
English: Wow! Neat!

Japanese: イトノコさん、早く！　早く！
English: Well, Detective Gumshoe? What are you waiting for?

Japanese: まま、そうアワてちゃダメッス。
English: Now, now, everything in due time, pal.

Japanese: お皿にたらしたクスリの色、 ハッキリと変わったッスね‥‥。
English: Well, the testing solution on the plate changed color alright.

Japanese: どうやらこの皿には、 睡眠薬が‥‥。
English: So there were sleeping pills on the plate!

Japanese: そういうことッスね。
English: Looks like it.

Japanese: 証拠品《ステーキの皿》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Steak Plate added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 捜査の方は、 どうなっているんですか？
English: How goes your investigation?

Japanese: ジッサイのところ、 大さわぎッス。
English: To tell the truth, it's a real mess, pal.

Japanese: このまま荷星を追求するべきだ という意見と、
English: Some people think we should pursue the case we already have against Powers...

Japanese: 容疑者を切りかえるべきだという 意見で、まっぷたつッス。
English: And some people think we should switch suspects!

Japanese: イトノコさんの意見は どうなんですか？
English: What do you think, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: くやしいッスが‥‥。
English: Well, pal, I hate to admit it...

Japanese: 自分は、荷星犯人説は 違うと思っているッス。
English: But I'm not sure I buy the case against Mr. Powers any more.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事には、また モウシワケないことを‥‥。
English: I feel kind of bad for Mr. Edgeworth, though.

Japanese: そういえば、御剣検事は どうしてます？
English: How is Edgeworth doing, anyway?

Japanese: それがもう、大荒れッス！
English: Edgeworth is out of control!

Japanese: 控え室で、アツいコーヒーの入った 紙コップをひねりつぶしたッス。
English: He was in the waiting room and he crushed this paper cup with hot, hot coffee in it.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Whoa...

Japanese: もう、大ヤケドッス！
English: Talk about burns, pal!

Japanese: わあい。いいキミだぁ！
English: Wow! The fury of Edgeworth!

Japanese: “紙コップ”‥‥ねえ。
English: Yeah, crushing paper cups...
(Cups... bottles...?)

Japanese: そういえば、あの小ビン、 どうなりました？
English: Say, what ever happened with that empty bottle?

Japanese: ああ。睡眠薬のビン、ッスか。
English: Oh, the bottle of sleeping pills?

Japanese: アンタには、いいニュースッス。
English: Well, I got some good news for you about that.

Japanese: ビンから、被害者・衣袋　武志の 指紋が発見されたッス。
English: They found the victim, Jack Hammer's prints on the bottle.

Japanese: ‥‥ということは‥‥。
English: So that means...

Japanese: どうやら、荷星を眠らせたのは、 被害者だったみたいッス。
English: Yep, it sounds like the one who put Powers to sleep was none other than... the victim!

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (So I was right!)

Japanese: 証拠品《睡眠薬の小ビン》のデータ を法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Sleeping Pill Bottle added to the Court Record.

Japanese: そういえば、御剣検事は どうしてます？
English: How is Edgeworth doing, anyway?

Japanese: それがもう、大荒れッス！
English: Edgeworth is out of control!

Japanese: 控え室で、アツいコーヒーの入った 紙コップをひねりつぶしたッス。
English: He was in the waiting room and he crushed this paper cup with hot, hot coffee in it.
Whoa...

Japanese: もう、大ヤケドッス！
English: Talk about burns, pal!

Japanese: わあい。いいキミだぁ！
English: Wow! The fury of Edgeworth!

Japanese: どうやら、荷星　三郎は、 睡眠薬を飲まされたらしいッスな。
English: It's looking like Will Powers was drugged...

Japanese: くわしいことは、これから署に 持って帰って調べるッス。
English: I'm going to go back to the precinct to do some more detailed tests.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか今日は、妙に スナオですね、イトノコさん‥‥。
English: You're being very... free with your information today, Detective.

Japanese: ここだけの話ッスが‥‥。
English: Well... don't go telling anyone this, pal.

Japanese: 今日の法廷、自分はチョッピリ、 カンドーしたッス。
English: But I was kind of impressed at today's trial, too.

Japanese: ナミの弁護士なら、カントクの 証言で、あきらめているッス。
English: Most lawyers would've given up after that director's testimony.

Japanese: あれがアンタだったから、 荷星　三郎は助かったッス。
English: I think if it'd been anyone but you up there, Powers would be done for by now.

Japanese: この小ビンですけど‥‥。
English: Detective, about that bottle...

Japanese: いや、おハズカシイッス。
English: Oh, that? Talk about embarrassing, pal!

Japanese: 最初の捜査で、そんなものを 見落とすなんて。
English: I can't believe I missed that on my first sweep for evidence.

Japanese: 御剣検事に、なんてオワビ すればいいのやら‥‥。
English: Don't know how I can apologize to Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 刑事さんも、 タイヘンなんですね‥‥。
English: It must be tough being a detective, with all that responsibility...

Japanese: ‥‥とほほ。
English: Oh, heh heh. *blush*

Japanese: ‥‥それについて知ってることは、 たぶんアンタと同じッス。
English: I'm guessing I know as much about that as you do, pal, i.e. nothing.

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコさん、 帰っちゃったのかな‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe must have left by now.

Japanese: 明日の裁判をどうするか、 考えているんじゃないか。
English: He's probably thinking about what to do in tomorrow's trial.

Japanese: また、御剣検事に おこられてるのかな。
English: I bet Edgeworth is pretty angry with him.

Japanese: １０月１９日　某時刻 撮影所・楽屋
English: October 19 Global Studios Dressing Room

Japanese: ‥‥誰もいない楽屋って、 なんかさびしいね。
English: Empty dressing rooms like this are kinda lonely, aren't they?

Japanese: 荷星さん、もうすぐ 帰ってこられるから、
English: I'm sure Mr. Powers will be back soon.

Japanese: だいじょうぶだと 思うよ、たぶん。
English: He'll be fine...

Japanese: ‥‥うん。
English: Y-yeah...

Japanese: 荷星さんのカバンだ。
English: Mr. Powers's bag.

Japanese: 早く取りに来られるといいね、 ニボサブさん。
English: I hope WP can come pick it up himself, soon.

Japanese: もうすぐだよ。
English: Soon, soon.

Japanese: 誰かが寝たあとが 残っている。
English: Looks like someone was sleeping here.

Japanese: ニボサブさん、やっぱり ヒルネしてたんだね。
English: Well, we can be pretty sure WP did take a nap here, like he said.

Japanese: 睡眠薬を飲まされて、はたして “ヒルネ”と言えるかどうか‥‥。
English: I'm not sure being drugged counts as taking a "nap"...

Japanese: あ。‥‥こんにちは。
English: Oh! Hello!

Japanese: どうしたの？ こんなところで。
English: What are you doing here?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥楽屋のかたづけを、 ちょっとね。
English: Oh... I was just cleaning up the dressing room.

Japanese: もう、ニボサブさんが 使うこともないし。
English: I guess WP won't be using it anymore, so...

Japanese: え？　え？ ‥‥どうして！
English: Wha!? What!? Why!?

Japanese: ニボサブさん、明日、 ムザイになるんだよ！
English: But WP's innocent!

Japanese: それは、本当にありがとう ございます。
English: Yes, and I'm really grateful for all you've done.

Japanese: でも‥‥。
English: But...

Japanese: トノサマン、今週で 最終回なんですよ‥‥。
English: This week is the last episode of the Steel Samurai...

Japanese: ええええええええええええええっ！
English: What!? Nooooooooooooooooo!

Japanese: 撮影用の和服にまじって、 洋服もちらほら。
English: There are some regular suits in among the costumes here.

Japanese: 荷星さんの私服だろうか。
English: Those must be his own clothes.

Japanese: カギがかかっている。
English: It's locked.

Japanese: あまり開けられることは ないみたいだ。
English: I guess he doesn't open his window much.

Japanese: まあ、そとはホコリっぽいから、 ムリもないかもしれないな。
English: It is kind of dusty outside.

Japanese: スタッフからの差し入れらしい おかしのハコがある。
English: There's a box for snacks on the table. They must give these to the employees.

Japanese: 中身はすでに、なくなっている。
English: Hmm. It's completely empty.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんのスルドイ視線を クビスジに感じる。
English: I can feel Maya's eyes burning into the back of my neck.

Japanese: 荷星さんのカバンだ。
English: It's Powers's bag.

Japanese: ニボサブさんの私物は、 ぜんぶ入れておきました。
English: I put all of WP's personal belongings in there.

Japanese: 取りに来られますよね？
English: He'll be able to come pick them up, right?

Japanese: ええ。 本人が、かならずね。
English: I'm sure he'll come, yes.

Japanese: 楽屋といえば、かならずある カガミだ。
English: They always have mirrors like this in dressing rooms.

Japanese: カガミを見ると、ぼくはかならず ヘアスタイルをチェックする。
English: I always check my hairdo when I look in a mirror.

Japanese: うーん。今日のとがりぐあいは いまひとつだな‥‥。
English: Hmm... not quite as spiky as usual, I see.

Japanese: メイク用の小道具なんかが ならんでいる。
English: There's an array of cosmetics here.

Japanese: カツラは“ちょんまげブギ” という番組で使われているものだ。
English: This is the wig used in "Samurai Boogey-Woogey."

Japanese: ‥‥おっ。 おぼえたね、なるほどくん。
English: Wow! You remembered, Nick!

Japanese: おかげさまで‥‥。
English: Thanks to you...

Japanese: 誰かが寝たあとが 残っている。
English: Looks like someone was sleeping here.

Japanese: あ。‥‥そこはまだ かたづけてないんです。
English: Oh, sorry. I haven't cleaned up there yet.

Japanese: イブクロさんのこと なんですけど‥‥。
English: I was wondering if I could ask you about Mr. Hammer...

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: 事件当日、あの人、第２スタジオに 行っているんですよ。
English: On the day of the murder, he went to Studio Two.

Japanese: え？　第１スタジオじゃなかった んですか？
English: Huh? I thought he went to Studio One?

Japanese: しかも、しかも、トノサマンの 着ぐるみを盗んでいるんだよ！
English: He stole the Steel Samurai costume, too!

Japanese: ええっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: イブクロさん、なんで そんなことを‥‥。
English: Why would Mr. Hammer do something like that?

Japanese: やっぱり。
English: Oh...

Japanese: あのウワサ、本当だったのかな。
English: I guess the rumor must be true, then.
The rumor?

Japanese: イブクロさん、姫神プロデューサー に、何か弱みをにぎられていて、
English: Yeah. Ms. Vasquez, that producer, had some kind of hold on Hammer.

Japanese: なんでも言いなりになって いたみたいなんですよ‥‥。
English: She had some dirt on him... He'd do anything she said, apparently.

Japanese: “弱み”‥‥って？
English: Some "dirt"?

Japanese: 教えて！
English: What dirt!

Japanese: 弱みというのは、その人の うしろぐらい部分、弱点です。
English: Dirt. You know, bad stuff.

Japanese: 知ってるよ、そんなこと。
English: Um, I know what dirt means...

Japanese: ５年前、ここでイブクロさん主演の 映画を撮っていたんですよ。
English: About five years ago, they were filming a movie starring Mr. Hammer.

Japanese: 当時、できたばっかりだった 第２スタジオで。
English: They were using the new studio... Studio Two.

Japanese: その撮影中に、何か事故が あったらしいんです。
English: Some sort of accident happened during filming.

Japanese: それ以来、第２スタジオは 使われなくなっちゃって。
English: After that, they never used Studio Two again.

Japanese: そのときのセットが、今も あるんです。
English: They left the film set the way it was, too.

Japanese: （セット‥‥？ あのコテージ、か‥‥）
English: (The film set...? Was that trailer part of the film set, then?)

Japanese: ５年前の事故‥‥ですか。
English: An accident five years ago?

Japanese: ええ。第２スタジオで。
English: Yes, in Studio Two.

Japanese: ‥‥あのコテージは、 当時のセットです。
English: That trailer there was part of the film set.

Japanese: その事故のこと、 教えてくれないかな。
English: Could you tell us what happened?

Japanese: スミマセン。
English: S-sorry.

Japanese: それだけは、言えないんです。
English: I don't think I can.

Japanese: きびしく口止めされて いますから。
English: They really don't want us to talk about it.

Japanese: 聞かせてくれないか。 ５年前、何があったか‥‥。
English: Could you tell us what happened five years ago?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。私の聞いていることを お話しします。
English: Well, I can tell you what I've heard.

Japanese: ５年前の撮影で、イブクロさん、 人を‥‥死なせたみたいなんです。
English: Apparently, five years ago, someone died... and it was Hammer's fault.

Japanese: え、ひ‥‥人を‥‥？
English: S-someone died!?

Japanese: あ！　モチロン 事故だったんですよ！
English: It was an accident, of course!

Japanese: それで‥‥。
English: Anyway...

Japanese: うまく立ち回って、もみ消したのが 姫神プロデューサーだったんです。
English: The producer, Ms. Vasquez managed to hush it up.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、イブクロさんの “弱み”ですか‥‥。
English: And that's the "dirt" she had on Mr. Hammer?

Japanese: だからイブクロさん、姫神 プロデューサーの言いなりに‥‥。
English: And that's why Mr. Hammer would do anything Ms. Vasquez asked him to do!

Japanese: でも、事故だったんなら、 公表しちゃえばよかったのに。
English: But, it was an accident, right? Why didn't they just make it public?

Japanese: 当時、イブクロさんは 大スターでしたから、
English: Well, you know Mr. Hammer was a big star back then.

Japanese: なんとしても かくしておきたかったんです。
English: They were afraid about what would happen to his career if word got out.

Japanese: そうだったんだ‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: 警備員のオバチャンなら、 もっとくわしく知っています。
English: You know who knows more about this? Ms. Oldbag.

Japanese: そのころから、この撮影所に いましたから‥‥。
English: She was here at the studios back then, you know.

Japanese: ありがとう。 ‥‥聞いてみるよ。
English: Thanks, We'll try asking her.

Japanese: （あのオバチャン、話して くれるかな‥‥）
English: (If she'll talk...)

Japanese: ト、トノサマン、終わっちゃうの！
English: W-what do you mean, the "last Steel Samurai"!?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。 でも、しょうがないですよ。
English: I know it's a shock, but nothing can be done to save it now.

Japanese: 人を死なせてしまったんですから。 ‥‥現実の世界で。
English: Whoever really did kill Mr. Hammer, also signed the Steel Samurai's death warrant.

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: No... no!

Japanese: それに、この英都撮影所では、
English: Also, I hear that Global Studios is going to change its programming.

Japanese: これからは、もういっさい、 子供向けの番組は作りません。
English: They're not going to make kids' shows anymore.

Japanese: え、ええっ！ ‥‥ど、どうして！
English: W-what!? Why!?

Japanese: 私もさびしいんですけど。
English: I don't know.

Japanese: でも、それが英都撮影所の 方針なんです‥‥。
English: It's sad, but that's what Global Studios decided.

Japanese: どうして子供番組を 作らないのかな？
English: Why aren't they going to make kids' shows anymore?

Japanese: 英都のエラい人たちは、 トノサマンを‥‥
English: The studio bigwigs don't want the Steel Samurai around anymore.

Japanese: この事件のことを、 わすれたいんです。
English: They want to forget all this ever happened.

Japanese: 誰にも触れられたく ないんですよ。
English: They want it quiet, you understand?

Japanese: そ、そんなの、勝手な 話だよ‥‥。
English: H-how can they just do that!

Japanese: トノサマンが好きな子供たちは どうすればいいの！
English: What about all the kids who love the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: だいじょうぶですよ。 きっと子供たちだって、
English: It's okay. I'm sure the kids will be fine.

Japanese: すぐに新しいヒーローを 見つけますから。
English: They'll find a new hero to follow.

Japanese: そんなことないよ！
English: No, that can't be true!

Japanese: 今、いきなり番組が終わったら、 みんな悲しむよ。
English: If the program just ends, they'll be heartbroken for sure!

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん！ なんか言ってよ！
English: N-Nick! Say something!

Japanese: え！ そ、そうだなあ‥‥。
English: Umm... yeah.

Japanese: （ここは、コトバで言うより、 証拠品のチカラを借りようか）
English: (I think evidence, rather than words, is called for here.)

Japanese: ちょっといいかな。 ‥‥これを見てほしいんだけど。
English: Here, take a look at this.

Japanese: ‥‥これが、どうかしましたか。
English: What's this?

Japanese: だから、トノサマンを やめないでほしいんだ。
English: This is why you can't stop the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: ‥‥ごめんなさい。 意味がわかりません。
English: I'm sorry... I don't understand.

Japanese: あたしも。
English: Neither do I, Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、ぼくもだ。 （‥‥もう一度、やってみるか）
English: F-funny that. I don't either! (Oops. Let's try that again.)
And this is...?

Japanese: ファンの子が、たった１人で 集めた写真だよ。
English: A fan collected all of these photographs.

Japanese: 子供たちは、トノサマンが 大好きなんだ。
English: The kids love the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: オトナたちのつごうで、 いきなり打ち切りなんて、
English: The show shouldn't be cancelled just so some adults can save face!

Japanese: スタッフなら、そんなことを しちゃあダメだよ。
English: I would think you would understand that better than anyone.

Japanese: そう、なんですよね‥‥。
English: You're right...

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました！
English: Okay!

Japanese: 私にできること、ありますか？
English: But what can I do?

Japanese: あるよ。
English: Well, for starters...

Japanese: 聞かせてほしいんだ。
English: You can tell me something.

Japanese: そこまでしてかくしたがる、 英都撮影所のヒミツを。
English: Tell me what it is that Global Studios is so intent on hiding?

Japanese: “５年前の事故”のことを 教えてくれないか。
English: Tell me about this accident five years ago.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました！
English: Okay!

Japanese: もう、トノサマン、 見られないんだ‥‥。
English: So... no more Steel Samurai?

Japanese: 撮影所の方針ですからね。
English: That's what the studio decided.

Japanese: 英都のエラい人たちは、 トノサマンを‥‥、
English: The studio bigwigs want to forget the Steel Samurai...

Japanese: いえ。この事件のことを わすれたいんです。
English: Rather, they want to forget this incident.

Japanese: 誰にも触れられたく ないんですよ。
English: They want things quiet.

Japanese: この写真‥‥イブクロさん、 だったんですか‥‥。
English: So this photo was Mr. Hammer all along?

Japanese: そうです。
English: It seems that way.

Japanese: イブクロさん‥‥ なんでそんなことを‥‥？
English: Why would Mr. Hammer do something like that?

Japanese: それは‥‥？
English: What's that?

Japanese: 睡眠薬のビンです。
English: An empty bottle of sleeping pills.

Japanese: ‥‥イブクロさんの指紋が ついています。
English: With Mr. Hammer's fingerprints on it...

Japanese: 私‥‥もう、何が なんだか‥‥。
English: Now I'm really confused!

Japanese: わあ‥‥すごい。
English: Wow... wow!

Japanese: これだけ集めるの、 タイヘンだったでしょうね。
English: It must have taken him forever to collect all these.

Japanese: 子供たちは、トノサマンが 大好きなんだよ。
English: Kids love the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: ‥‥私もうれしいです。
English: They do, don't they?

Japanese: ごめんなさい‥‥。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: あの。思い当たること、 ありません。
English: I'm not sure what to make of that.

Japanese: スタッフの子、 どこか行っちゃったね。
English: The assistant must have gone off somewhere.

Japanese: イブクロさんの話を聞いて、 ショックだったのかな。
English: That stuff about Mr. Hammer must have come as a shock to her.

Japanese: １０月１９日　某時刻 第１スタジオ前
English: October 19 Studio One Entrance

Japanese: 事件があった日‥‥
English: Think back, Nick, to the day of the murder...

Japanese: イブクロさん、トノサマンの 着ぐるみを着て、
English: Mr. Hammer put on the Steel Samurai costume.

Japanese: ここから第２スタジオに 行ったんだよね‥‥？
English: Then he left from here to go to Studio Two...?

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Right.

Japanese: でも、なんのためかなあ。
English: But why?

Japanese: 姫神プロデューサーか、カントクが 呼び出したのかなあ‥‥。
English: I wonder if someone called him...? Like the director, or the producer...?

Japanese: 第１スタジオへ通じる入口。
English: The entrance to Studio One.

Japanese: スタジオは、衣袋　武志が 死体で見つかった現場だ。
English: This is the studio where they found Jack Hammer's body.

Japanese: カードキーがないので、 今日は入れない。
English: I don't have that cardkey anymore, so I can't go in there today.

Japanese: この奥には、 第２スタジオがある。
English: Studio Two is down that way.

Japanese: 本当の殺人現場は、 実は第２スタジオだったんだ。
English: Studio Two... the real scene of the crime.

Japanese: ねえねえ、なんで そんなことがわかったの？
English: Say, Nick, how did you figure that out again?

Japanese: それはね‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: 長くなるから、また今度。
English: Actually, it's a long story, I'll tell you another time.

Japanese: ううう。
English: Aww.

Japanese: この撮影スタジオの マスコット・キャラクターだ。
English: The mascot of Global Studios.

Japanese: “サルマゲどん”というらしい。
English: Apparently its name is "Mr. Monkey."

Japanese: このゲートを通った人間を 自動的に撮影するカメラだ。
English: This security camera takes photos of everyone who comes through the gate.

Japanese: どうやら今日は 作動してないみたいだ。
English: It doesn't seem to be switched on today.

Japanese: ちょっとつかれたな。 ‥‥座って休もうか。
English: I'm bushed. Maybe I'll sit down here for a bit.

Japanese: あーあ。これだから トシはとりたくないよね。
English: This is exactly why I don't want to get old, ever.

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくはまだ ワカいぞ！
English: H-hey! I'm still young! I'm just tired!

Japanese: なるほどくん。“若さ”を 主張しだしたら、オシマイだよ。
English: Nick, when you start having to say "I'm still young," you know it's all over.

Japanese: （いやいや、ほんとうに 若いんだってば！）
English: (But... but I AM still young!)

Japanese: 『おいでませ』だって。
English: It says "Welcome."

Japanese: 休みの日は、一般の人たちが 遊びに来るからね。
English: They let people in here when they're not filming, right?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 今度、遊びに来ようか。
English: Nick! Let's come here some day! Whaddya say?

Japanese: あのオバチャンが 入れてくれたら、ね。
English: Sure, if Ms. Oldbag lets us in.

Japanese: １０月１９日　某時刻 第２スタジオ前
English: October 19 Studio Two Entrance

Japanese: あ‥‥。なるほどくん、 姫神プロデューサーだよ‥‥。
English: Hey, Nick... It's Ms. Vasquez!

Japanese: こんにちは。
English: Hello?

Japanese: こ・ん・に・ち・は・！
English: H-E-L-L-O!

Japanese: こ！
English: H!

Japanese: ん！
English: E!

Japanese: に！
English: L!

Japanese: ち！
English: L!

Japanese: は！
English: O!

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃん！ ムキになっちゃダメだって。
English: M-Maya! You should at least try to be polite...

Japanese: 雲を見てるの。アタクシ。
English: I'm watching the clouds.

Japanese: （終わりみたいだな‥‥）
English: (That's all she had to say, apparently...)

Japanese: スタッフ移動用の、 大型のライトバンだ。
English: A light van for moving staff and equipment around.

Japanese: よく手入れされていて、 免許さえあれば、すぐ動かせる。
English: They've kept it in pretty good shape. Looks like it'd run fine.

Japanese: え！　じゃあ、じゃあ、 ドライブしようよ、撮影所１周。
English: Well, let's go for a drive!

Japanese: 免許があれば、の話だよ。
English: Sure... if I had a license.

Japanese: うわあ。なるほどくん。 免許、持ってないんだ。
English: Nick! You don't have a driver's license!?

Japanese: そのかわりに、ホラ。
English: But, look, I have this!

Japanese: こんなところで弁護士バッジ つきつけたって、ダメだよ！
English: Why are you showing me your attorney's badge, Nick?

Japanese: 宇在カントクと姫神プロデューサー の食事のあとだな。
English: This is where Sal Manella and Dee Vasquez ate.

Japanese: 皿の上には、何も残っていない。
English: There's nothing left on the plates.

Japanese: あ。この人たちも食べたんだね、 ホネつきステーキ。
English: Hey, so they ate t-bone steaks too.

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: Something wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥なんかちょっと、 ヘンな気がするなあ‥‥。
English: I don't know... I'm getting this funny feeling.

Japanese: イワ感、っていうの？ なんだろ、この感じ‥‥。
English: Something about this just feels... odd, you know?
Can't say that I do...

Japanese: 大きく“２”と書いてある。
English: There's a big "2" painted here.

Japanese: 九太くんの写真に写っていたのは、 ここの数字だな。
English: So this is the number in Cody's photograph.

Japanese: 今日は、こいつのおかげで 助かったなあ‥‥。
English: And to think, if he hadn't brought that photograph in...

Japanese: なるほどくん。 シミジミするのは早いよ。
English: Don't get all teary eyed yet.

Japanese: 大きく“２”と書いてある。
English: There's a big "2" painted here.

Japanese: 九太くんの写真に写っていたのは、 ここの数字だな。
English: So this is the number in Cody's photograph.

Japanese: 今日は、こいつのおかげで 助かったなあ‥‥。
English: And to think, if he hadn't brought that photograph in...

Japanese: なるほどくん。 シミジミするのは早いよ。
English: Don't get all teary eyed yet.

Japanese: 最近、取りつけられたらしい 小さな焼却炉だ。
English: An incinerator. Apparently they just put this one in.

Japanese: コゲたススがこびりついていて、 あまり手入れされていない。
English: It's already covered in soot. They must not clean it much.

Japanese: 撮影セットを組むための 資材みたいだけど‥‥。
English: These look like materials for making a movie set.

Japanese: ずいぶん長いこと、 置きっぱなしになっているみたい。
English: They've been sitting here for quite some time.

Japanese: まるで、資材置き場だね。
English: This might as well be a junkyard.

Japanese: よく見ると、建てられてから 数年はたっているようだ。
English: On closer inspection, this trailer looks like it's been here for a while.

Japanese: これはもう、“セット”なんて もんじゃなさそうだ。
English: I don't think this is part of some temporary film set.

Japanese: 何やら花が咲いている。
English: There're some flowers here.

Japanese: わあ、キレイだねー。 ちゃんと手入れされてるよ。
English: Wow, so pretty! They're taking good care of these.

Japanese: あんまり近づくなよ。 その鉄柵、あぶないから。
English: Don't get too close. That fence looks dangerous.

Japanese: コテージへ入るドアだ。 カギはかかっていない。
English: The trailer door. It's unlocked.

Japanese: 名もなき花が咲いている。
English: Nameless flowers bloom in solitude.

Japanese: もしかしたら名前は あるのかもしれないが、
English: They probably have a proper name...

Japanese: ぼくは一生、知ることなく 終わるのだなあ。
English: But it is not for me to know. Not now, nor ever till the end of my days.

Japanese: イミもなく しみじみしてしまった。
English: I need a vacation.

Japanese: あの。あの。事件当日、 イブクロさんはここへ‥‥
English: Um, excuse me? Mr. Hammer came here on the day of the murder, and...

Japanese: シッ。
English: Shh.

Japanese: 聞こえなかったかしら。
English: Perhaps you didn't hear me.

Japanese: 雲を見ているの、アタクシ。
English: I'm watching the clouds.

Japanese: 他のことに、興味はないわ。
English: I'm not interested in talking to you.

Japanese: なるほどくーん！ ムカつくよぉー！
English: Do something, Nick! She's really ticking me off!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Oh dear...)

Japanese: 荷星さん、ハンニンじゃ なかったんですよ！
English: Umm... Mr. Powers wasn't the killer!

Japanese: これでまた、トノサマン、 つづけられますね！
English: You don't have to cancel the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: もうあきたわ、アタクシ。 ‥‥トノサマンは。
English: I think I'm tired of the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: ハ‥‥‥ハンニンだ。
English: You... you did it!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ コイツがハンニンだよ！
English: Nick! She did it! She's the killer!

Japanese: あたしの、綾里家の霊力が そう言ってるゥ！
English: My Fey blood speaks to me! I know it! I know it!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Oh dear...)

Japanese: あの、宇在カントクの ことですけど‥‥。
English: Um, I was wondering if you could tell me about Mr. Manella?

Japanese: どうしても あの男の話をしたいのなら、
English: If you must talk about that man...

Japanese: コテージの中で やってもらえるかしら。
English: Perhaps you could talk about it in the trailer?

Japanese: アタクシはヌキで。
English: By yourselves?

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Oh, oh dear...)

Japanese: （‥‥見ようともしない）
English: (She's not even looking.)

Japanese: それ。
English: Where.

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい？
English: Wh-what?

Japanese: どこで拾ったの？
English: Where did you get that?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥。
English: I, well...

Japanese: 大場ね。
English: Oldbag.

Japanese: お、おおば‥‥？
English: O-Old...?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 警備員のオバチャンの名前だよ。
English: She means the security lady, Ms. Oldbag, Nick.

Japanese: と、とにかく。
English: Right... anyway...

Japanese: 姫神さん。
English: Ms. Vasquez.

Japanese: あなたは、この事故を もみ消すかわりに、
English: You hid this incident from the press, didn't you?

Japanese: イブクロさんを、自由に あやつっていたんですね！
English: And you used it to control Mr. Hammer!

Japanese: 風。
English: The wind.

Japanese: え。‥‥な、なんですか？
English: The... huh?

Japanese: 強くなってきたわね、風が。
English: It's gotten stronger, don't you think? The wind.

Japanese: おもしろい話。
English: Your conversation interests me.

Japanese: つづきは、コテージでゆっくり 聞かせてもらうわ。
English: Let's talk about it more, inside the trailer.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 コテージの中に入っちゃったよ。
English: Nick. She went inside the trailer.

Japanese: （‥‥見ようともしない）
English: (She's not even looking.)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、ほら。
English: Okay. Hey, you look.

Japanese: な、なんであたしに 見せるの？
English: Wh-why are you showing that to me, Nick?

Japanese: １０月１９日　某時刻 第２スタジオ・コテージ内
English: October 19 Studio Two Trailer

Japanese: 昨日、あのスタッフの子が そうじしてたけど、
English: Wasn't the assistant cleaning up yesterday?

Japanese: あんまりキレイになってないね。
English: Funny, it still seems pretty messy in here.

Japanese: トノサマンのレアカードを 手に入れて、浮かれてたからなあ。
English: I guess she was still on a high from getting that rare card she needed.

Japanese: 古びたポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: “ダイナマイト侍 主演、衣袋　武志”か。
English: "'Dynamite Samurai' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: 被害者のイブクロさん、 昔はすごかったんだなあ。
English: I guess Mr. Hammer used to be quite the star.

Japanese: “武者ぶるいの夏 主演、衣袋　武志”。
English: "'Samurai Summer' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: これまたずいぶん 古いポスターだな。
English: This poster's quite old.

Japanese: あー、これ！ なつかしいなあ。
English: Wow! It's been years since I saw that show!

Japanese: 誰にでもある“ひと夏の ロマンス”がテーマなんだよね。
English: It was one of those typical summer romance stories... but it was great!

Japanese: テーマはともかく、それがなんで サムライにウマなんだよ。
English: A typical summer romance... with a samurai on a horse?

Japanese: 昔の映画のポスターだな。 “武士節　主演、衣袋　武志”
English: An old movie poster. "'The Singing Samurai' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: “武士節”って、やっぱり “ぶしぶし”って読むのかな。
English: What the hell do samurai sing about? Chopping off heads?

Japanese: あー、それ！
English: Oh. My. God.

Japanese: ジマンのノドで大スターに のし上がる、武士の話だよ。
English: That movie was the best! It's about a samurai who sings his way to fame and fortune!

Japanese: イブクロさん、 歌もうまかったからね。
English: Mr. Hammer was a very accomplished singer, you know.

Japanese: スターにのし上がる話なのに、 なんで時代が室町なんだよ‥‥。
English: Singing samurai stars... What's next? Ninja ballet?

Japanese: ‥‥特に手がかりになるものは 入っていないな。
English: Nothing in here that could be a clue.

Japanese: 紙コップとか、丸めた資料 なんかが捨ててあるだけだ。
English: Just some paper cups and crumpled documents.

Japanese: ここで会議をしていたらしい。
English: This is where they had that meeting.

Japanese: 机の上には、資料にお茶、 紙コップなどが並んでいる。
English: There are documents and paper cups scattered around.

Japanese: くもりガラスで外は見えない。
English: The glass is frosted. I can't see outside.

Japanese: １０月も半ばをすぎて、 陽ざしもすっかりやわらいできた。
English: This far into October, the dimming daylight makes it feel like winter's coming.

Japanese: トノサマンで使うセットの デザインを決めていたようだ。
English: It looks like they were designing a set to use for the Steel Samurai show.

Japanese: セットを組む予算なんかも メモされている。
English: They've got some figures for how much the set costs...

Japanese: ‥‥なんでこんなに お金がかかるんだ‥‥？
English: I can't believe they spend that much money on those sets!

Japanese: フン‥‥来たね。
English: Hmph. You came.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、つづきを 聞かせてもらおうじゃないか。
English: Well then, what was that you were talking about?

Japanese: （‥‥！ なんか、フンイキが違うぞ‥‥）
English: Why is she so eager to talk all of a sudden?)

Japanese: 姫神さん。
English: Ms. Vasquez.

Japanese: あなたは、この事件をネタに、
English: You were using that accident!

Japanese: イブクロさんを 自由にあやつっていたんですね！
English: You were blackmailing Mr. Hammer so you could control him, weren't you?

Japanese: イブクロさんは、格安の出演料で 子供番組の悪役を‥‥！
English: That's why he was doing kids' shows for petty change!
Hmph...

Japanese: 人を脅迫者呼ばわりかい‥‥？
English: So I'm a blackmailer now?

Japanese: だって、あなたのやったことは、 そうじゃないですか！
English: Well, that's what it was, wasn't it!

Japanese: たしかに、事故で人を死なせて しまったけど‥‥、
English: I mean, sure, it was an accident...

Japanese: それをネタに、イブクロさんを スターの座から引きずり下ろして！
English: But you used it to drag Mr. Hammer down from his rightful place as a star!

Japanese: ‥‥お待ち。
アタクシは、引きずり下ろすなんて ことは、してないよ。
English: I haven't pulled anyone down from anywhere.

Japanese: イブクロのやつが、勝手に ダメになっていっただけの話さ。
English: Mr. Hammer's career went sour of its own accord.

Japanese: で、でも！
English: B-but!

Japanese: その原因になったのは、 あなたじゃないんですか！
English: You were the cause! You pressured him...

Japanese: 不運な事故だったのに‥‥
English: And to think it was just an accident...!

Japanese: ちょいとッ！
English: Excuse me!

Japanese: ‥‥ナニさ？
English: What is this all about?

Japanese: さっきから“事故”“事故”って。
English: You keep saying "accident," "accident"...

Japanese: ‥‥どうして“事故”だと 決めつけるんだい？
English: How are you so sure?

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: ものわかりが悪いねえ‥‥。
English: Must I spell everything out for you?

Japanese: 事故じゃなかったら、それは何さ？ 考えてみるんだね‥‥。
English: Think: what would it be if it wasn't an accident?
No...

Japanese: まさか‥‥。
English: No way!

Japanese: イブクロさんは‥‥ “故意”に‥‥？
English: You mean Mr. Hammer did it... on purpose?

Japanese: ああ。そうさ！
English: That is what I mean.

Japanese: 証拠は！　‥‥その証拠は あるんですか！
English: Wh-where's your proof! Can you prove it!?

Japanese: ‥‥フン！
English: Hmph!

Japanese: ‥‥そうじゃなかったら‥‥
English: Just think!

Japanese: ５年間も、 人の言いなりになんか、なるかい？
English: Would he have let me run his life for five years... over a mere accident?

Japanese: アタクシはこの５年、 アイツをアゴで使ってきたんだよ。
English: And I ran him hard, believe you me!

Japanese: で、でも！　警備員のオバチャン は、あれは“事故”だったって！
English: B-but, the security lady said it was an accident!

Japanese: ああ。あの女は、イブクロの ファンだったからねえ。
English: Oh. Well, she was a big fan of Hammer's, you see.

Japanese: 写真を持って撮影所にやってきた 記者に飛びかかっていって、
English: She jumped on the reporter who brought that photo into the studio.

Japanese: ボコボコになぐられながら、 その写真を奪いとったぐらいだ。
English: She wrenched it out of his hands, she did. Gave him a few bruises, too.

Japanese: （だからオバチャン、 写真を持っていたんだ‥‥）
English: (So that's why she had the photo...)

Japanese: バカな女さ。
English: She's an old fool.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなもの、ネガさえあれば いくらでも現像できるのにねえ。
English: Of course, all the reporter would need is the negatives. He could have made a copy.

Japanese: でも。
English: But he didn't.

Japanese: 今となっては、この世に残っている 写真は、それ１枚だけだ。
English: The only copy of that photo is the one you hold.

Japanese: ‥‥わたしてもらおうか、それ。
English: Give it to me. Now.

Japanese: な、ナニ言ってるんですか！ これは、大切な証拠‥‥
English: W-what? This is valuable evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥アンタたちッ！
English: Boys!

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。
English: U-umm...

Japanese: こ‥‥この方たちは‥‥？
English: Who are they...?

Japanese: プロ。
English: Professionals.

Japanese: なんでもモミ消すのが こいつらのシゴトさ。
English: They're good at erasing... various things.

Japanese: オマエたちも、 モミ消してやろうか‥‥？
English: What do you think...? Would you like to be erased?

Japanese: な、何ッ！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 法廷は、明日が最終日、か。
English: The trial ends tomorrow?

Japanese: ザンネンだったねェ。
English: How unfortunate.

Japanese: 明日の法廷、オマエたちは 欠席してもらうよ。
English: It's a shame you'll have to miss it!

Japanese: な‥‥なぜ？
English: Tell me why.

Japanese: どうして、この写真に こだわるんだ？
English: Why do you want this photograph so badly?

Japanese: これは、“イブクロさん”の 弱みであって‥‥
English: This is Mr. Hammer's "dirt"... no?

Japanese: “姫神　サクラ”にとっては、 どうでもいいことなのに‥‥。
English: Why should Dee Vasquez care about it at all?

Japanese: 考える時間は、たっぷりあるさ。
English: I'm sure you'll have plenty of time to think about that where you're going.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ。 アバヨ、お２人さん‥‥。
English: So long, friends.

Japanese: 始末は、たのんだよ、アンタたち。
English: Boys, erase away.

Japanese: いやああああああああああああっ！
English: Nooooooooooooo!

Japanese: ちょっと待つッスゥ！
English: Hold it right there!

Japanese: ‥‥話は聞かせて もらったッス！
English: I heard everything, pal!

Japanese: 姫神　サクラ！
English: Dee Vasquez!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、署まで 来てもらうッス！
English: You're coming down to the precinct with me, now!

Japanese: フン。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: なかなかやるじゃないか、 ボウヤ。
English: Not bad.

Japanese: どうやら勝負は‥‥
English: Very well.

Japanese: 明日の法廷、ということに なりそうだね‥‥。
English: It appears this contest will be decided tomorrow then. In court.

Japanese: 楽しみにしておくよ。
English: I'll be looking forward to it.

Japanese: だ、だいじょぶッス？
English: H-hey, you okay, pal?

Japanese: ちょっと、踏み込むタイミングが おくれたッス。
English: Sorry I was a little late with my entrance.

Japanese: まだまだ、ベンキョウ不足ッス。
English: I don't get many chances to practice that sorta thing.

Japanese: 刑事さん‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: ありがとうございました！
English: Thank you thank you thank you!

Japanese: あたし‥‥ こわかったよおぉ。
English: I was really scared...

Japanese: ナニナニ。 ‥‥これが、シゴトッスから。
English: Huh? Don't mention it, pal. Just doing my job.

Japanese: どうしたんですか？
English: Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: 一度、このセリフが言って みたくて刑事になったッス‥‥。
English: Sorry, it's just... I've wanted to say that line ever since I became a detective.

Japanese: じゃあ、自分はこれから、 もうひと仕事あるッス。
English: Okay. I've got one more job to do today.

Japanese: 縁があったら、また会うッス！
English: I'm sure we'll run into each other again soon!

Japanese: さあ、なるほどくん。
English: Well, Nick.

Japanese: いよいよクライマックスだね。
English: It looks like we're getting close to the bottom of this.

Japanese: （姫神　サクラ‥‥）
English: (And who's at the bottom...?)

Japanese: （どうやら、ターゲットは 決まったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Dee Vasquez!)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.
